repo_notes.author: netzkontrast/agency maintainers
repo_notes.content:
Machine · Actor · Space Map (verbatim) — the conceptual core of this repository. Every Space (directory) has exactly one canonical Actor role and exactly one canonical Machine enforcement layer. Gaps in either column are governance debt.

| Space (directory) | Actor (canonical operator) | Machine (enforcement layer) |
|---|---|---|
| `/` root | Human maintainer | `tools/check-governance.sh`, `.githooks/pre-commit` |
| `/prompts/` | Human (intent author) + Claude Code | `tools/lint-linkage.py`, `tools/fm/validate` |
| `/research/` | Claude Code + External agents (Gemini) | `tools/lint-structure.py`, `tools/fm/validate` |
| `/tasks/` | Claude Code + Human reviewer | `tools/lint-linkage.py`, `tools/fm/validate`, `tools/check-task-lifecycle-classification.py` |
| `/decisions/` | Human architect + Claude Code | `tools/adr/` CLI (`agency-adr`) |
| `/skills/` | Human + Claude Code | `tools/fm/validate` |
| `/tools/` | Claude Code (implementation) | `tools/tests/` (pytest), `tools/lint-runlog.py` |
| `/Agency-System/` | Frontend/backend product layer | Separate from governance framework |
| `/maintenance/` | Claude Code (maintenance runs) | `tools/lint-runlog.py`, `tools/check-maintenance-bypass.py` |
| `/templates/` | Human + Claude Code | `tools/fm/validate` |

Isomorphism invariant: every Space has exactly one canonical Actor and one canonical Machine. The `/Agency-System/` row is intentionally non-isomorphic — it marks the boundary between the governance framework and a product layer that is OUT OF SCOPE for the audit graph (FOLDERS.md §8).

repo_notes.author: netzkontrast/agency maintainers
repo_notes.content: This repo is spec-first: AGENTS.md, TASK.md, RESEARCH.md, PROMPT.md, FOLDERS.md, SKILLS.md, FRUSTRATED.md, MAINTENANCE.md, PRE_COMMIT.md are the nine root governance specs. Every file in /prompts/, /research/, /tasks/ carries frontmatter that cross-references these specs. The tools/ layer mechanically enforces these cross-references via tools/check-governance.sh which runs at every commit through .githooks/pre-commit. The specs use RFC 2119 normative keywords (uppercase MUST / MUST NOT / SHOULD / MAY) and Gherkin acceptance criteria — they are normative contracts, not casual documentation.

repo_notes.author: netzkontrast/agency maintainers
repo_notes.content: The Prompt → Research → Task pipeline is the core workflow: a prompt in /prompts/ is executed by an agent to produce a workspace in /research/; coordination and sequencing lives in /tasks/. Each step requires frontmatter linkage validated by tools/lint-linkage.py. The audit-graph keys are: task_uses_prompts, task_spawns_research, prompt_relates_to_task, prompt_spawned_from_research, research_executes_prompt. The Task layer is the planning surface (low cost, tokens only); the Research layer is the execution surface (sandbox + git + ACUs). This separation is isomorphic to the Devin Ask/Agent planning-execution split.

repo_notes.author: netzkontrast/agency maintainers
repo_notes.content: The 032–039 amendment chain (May 2026) updated all eight root governance specs in a coordinated sequence. See tasks/032-039 and decisions/0001-0005 for the rationale. The amendment chain is bounded by a chain-level falsification gate: it MUST reduce friction, MUST NOT introduce new cross-spec contradictions (pre-chain baseline of 16 catalogued contradictions in research/research-cross-spec-contradiction-baseline/), and MUST close all briefing-pending subtasks. Do not interpret any single spec in isolation from this chain. Architectural decisions are append-only ADRs in /decisions/<NNNN>-<slug>.md (MADR 4.0.0); Accepted ADRs are T4-immutable and supersede via successor ADRs only. The <!-- BEGIN/END AGENCY-ADR SYNTHESIS --> block in AGENTS.md is rewritten by tools/adr/cli.py synthesize from the /decisions/ corpus — manual edits are overwritten.

repo_notes.author: netzkontrast/agency maintainers
repo_notes.content: Agency-System/ is a separate product layer (frontend HTML/JS + Python/FastAPI backend). It is NOT part of the governance framework. FOLDERS.md §8 exempts it from frontmatter requirements and from the audit graph. tools/validate-frontmatter.py and tools/lint-linkage.py do NOT walk this folder. Documentation generators MUST NOT treat Agency-System as core governance content; treat it as application code with its own README and TODO files.

pages.title: Repository Overview
pages.purpose: Elevator-pitch overview of netzkontrast/agency: a governance and orchestration substrate for long-horizon AI-agent work, NOT an application. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md §1 for the binding statement of purpose. State the four-way separation of concerns (Task / Prompt / Research / Skill) as the organising principle, citing the table in CLAUDE.md §1.

pages.title: How This Repo Works
pages.purpose: Explain the operational model: the Prompt → Research → Task pipeline, the spec-first discipline (RFC 2119 + Gherkin), the frontmatter audit graph as source of truth for cross-directory linkage. Reference PROMPT.md, RESEARCH.md §1-§6, TASK.md §1-§3. Position this repo as the planning/execution-separated counterpart to autonomous-agent platforms like Devin.

pages.title: How to Navigate
pages.purpose: A reader's map of the repository topology. Reference FOLDERS.md and the file list at the repo root (the nine root *.md governance specs plus CLAUDE.md, README.md, install.sh). Explain the convention `<NNN>-<slug>` for tasks, kebab-case slugs for prompts and research, and the readme.md rule that every operational folder MUST contain its own readme.md.

pages.title: Machine · Actor · Space Map
pages.purpose: The conceptual core. Render the verbatim ten-row table from repo_notes[0] as the page body, then narrate each row. Reference tools/check-governance.sh, tools/fm/validate, tools/adr/ (`agency-adr` CLI), and decisions/ as the canonical Machine enforcers. Explain why the /Agency-System/ row is deliberately non-isomorphic (boundary marker between governance and product layer).

pages.title: The Nine Root Specs
pages.purpose: List and characterise each of the nine root governance specs at the repo root: AGENTS.md, TASK.md, PROMPT.md, RESEARCH.md, FOLDERS.md, SKILLS.md, FRUSTRATED.md, MAINTENANCE.md, PRE_COMMIT.md. Reference each file by exact path. Emphasise that they are NORMATIVE CONTRACTS using RFC 2119 keywords and Gherkin scenarios — not casual documentation. Note that root-spec edits are T1/T2 mechanical repairs only; T3 structural changes go through ADRs.

pages.title: Session Protocol
pages.purpose: The mandatory session bootstrap: every agent runs ./install.sh first (installs PyYAML, jsonschema, pytest from tools/requirements.txt) and then tools/check-governance.sh BEFORE reading or writing any file. Reference AGENTS.md §Session Setup, install.sh, tools/check-governance.sh, .githooks/pre-commit. Explain the closing-run procedure (Claude Code MUST close every session with /sc:createPR after a successful git push).

pages.title: Frontmatter Schema
pages.purpose: The Layered Schema with Namespacing that every operational file MUST carry. Reference decisions/0003-frontmatter-source-of-truth.md (canonical decision), tools/fm/SPEC.md (toolchain contract), and TASK.md §3 (worked examples). Explain L0 (Obsidian-reserved), L1 (Vault Core: type, status, slug, summary, created, updated), L2 (domain namespaces: task_*, prompt_*, research_*, skill_*, adr_*), L3 (agent-only, NOT in YAML). Hard rule: YAML depth ≤ 1; mutate via tools/fm/edit.py, not sed/awk.

pages.title: Pre-Commit Enforcement
pages.purpose: Mechanical governance via the pre-commit hook. Reference PRE_COMMIT.md, .githooks/pre-commit, tools/check-governance.sh, tools/install-hooks.sh. Enumerate the linter pipeline: tools/fm/validate.py (frontmatter + headings + linkage), tools/lint-structure.py (required files), tools/lint-runlog.py (maintenance log), tools/adr/cli.py validate (ADR ledger). Note advisory-tier WARN linters: tools/check-rfc2119-polarity.py, tools/check-assumption-log.py, tools/check-narrative-ontology-load.py.

pages.title: Prompts — The Intent Layer
pages.purpose: What /prompts/ is and how to author one. Reference PROMPT.md and prompts/readme.md. Explain the mandatory three-file scaffold (prompt.md, brief.md, readme.md) per FOLDERS.md §4.1, and the prompt frontmatter namespace (prompt_kind, prompt_framework, prompt_target_agent, prompt_relates_to_task, prompt_spawned_from_research). Anchor: a prompt is the EXECUTABLE INSTRUCTION SET an agent runs; it MUST NOT be inlined inside a task.md.

pages.title: Research — The Evidence Layer
pages.purpose: What /research/ is and the workspace shape. Reference RESEARCH.md §2-§6 and research/readme.md. Explain the four subfolders (workspace/, synthesis/, reflection/, output/) and the immutable prompt snapshot at /research/<slug>/prompt.md. Cover external-research ingestion under /research/<provider>/<slug>/ per RESEARCH.md §6, and the binding rule that every external result.md MUST trigger a downstream analysis Task per §6.5. Cite this very repository's intake of the Gemini DeepWiki research as the canonical example.

pages.title: Tasks — The Coordination Layer
pages.purpose: What /tasks/ is and how Tasks orchestrate work. Reference TASK.md §3 and tasks/readme.md. Explain the <NNN>-<slug> naming convention, the task_status lifecycle (open → in_progress → done | abandoned), the L2 task_* namespace (task_uses_prompts, task_spawns_research, task_supersedes, task_superseded_by, task_blocked_by, task_affects_paths, task_owner, task_priority). Anchor: a Task is the goal-plan-todo unit; it links to its prompts via frontmatter and never inlines them.

pages.title: Decisions (ADRs)
pages.purpose: Architectural Decision Records as MADR 4.0.0 ledger. Reference decisions/0001-0005 (the first ratified five), tools/adr/ (`agency-adr` CLI), and the synthesis pipeline marker `<!-- BEGIN/END AGENCY-ADR SYNTHESIS -->` in AGENTS.md. Explain that Accepted ADRs are T4-immutable and supersede only via successor ADRs (DAG cycle-detection enforced). Cover the JSON-Schema and Gherkin acceptance criteria contract.

pages.title: Skills — Capability Registry
pages.purpose: What /skills/ is — version-controlled mirror of Claude skills (SKILL.md + assets). Reference SKILLS.md and skills/readme.md. Explain that runtime location is ~/.claude/skills/ and /mnt/skills/user/; per-skill readme.md files are auto-generated and SHOULD NOT be hand-edited. Cover the skill_* L2 frontmatter (skill_references_skills, skill_references_research, skill_references_prompts) and the Citation Reproducibility Protocol (path:Lstart-Lend@<sha>) for narrative-ontology contexts.

pages.title: Toolchain — The Machine Layer
pages.purpose: What /tools/ contains and how the linters compose. Reference tools/readme.md, tools/fm/ (canonical frontmatter toolchain — Python 3.11 stdlib only), tools/adr/ (ADR validate/synthesize/compress/graph), tools/dramatica-nav/ (narrative-ontology navigator), tools/legacy/ (one-release deprecation shims), tools/tests/ (pytest suites). Anchor: the toolchain is the ONLY place new infrastructure code is added; specs are normative contracts, /tools/ is the implementation.

pages.title: How We Got Here
pages.purpose: The history layer for human auditors. Reference decisions/0001-0005 (the ratified-Proposed transition), maintenance/run-log.md (every coherence run since 2026-05-04), and the Tasks 032–039 amendment chain (the May-2026 spec-corpus integration). Explain that the audit graph itself IS the compliance trail under EU AI Act and U.S. Algorithmic Accountability Act traceability requirements: every binding clause traces to an ADR or an amendment Task; every Task traces to a prompt and a research workspace; every research run logs friction (FL0–FL3) per FRUSTRATED.md.

pages.title: What Is Next
pages.purpose: The forward-looking horizon. Reference tasks/readme.md (open tasks, currently including 022, 023, 024, 025, 035–039, 042–046, 048–050) and the in-flight prompts under /prompts/. Explain known deferred items: the 10-ACU degradation budget (Tasks 044/047 follow-up); the optional in-house llms.txt (deferred until DeepWiki auto-generation lands); the open-source DeepWiki alternatives deepwiki-open / deepwiki-rs (deferred — revisit if cost or sovereignty constraints change). The horizon is intentionally short: the repo's amendment chains are the way the future is committed.
